# UC-22.1.35 · GDPR DPIA Remediation Monitoring and Mitigation Closure

## Description

The DPIA must include measures to address risks. This use case ties DPIA mitigation items to ITSM work orders, tracks age and overdue status, and verifies technical completion signals (e.g., control deployment logs) where available.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for GDPR compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:sc_task. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Export mitigation action list from each approved DPIA into `gdpr_dpia_mitigation_register.csv`; (2) create linked ServiceNow tasks with due dates; (3) alert owners weekly on overdue items; (4) optionally confirm technical controls via GitHub merge events or Terraform apply logs; (5) close DPIA only when all mitigations are verified. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:sc_task`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Add-on for GitHub (Splunkbase 5596).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `gdpr_dpia_mitigation_register.csv`, `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:sc_task"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Export mitigation action list from each approved DPIA into `gdpr_dpia_mitigation_register.csv`; (2) create linked ServiceNow tasks with due dates; (3) alert owners weekly on overdue items; (4) optionally confirm technical controls via GitHub merge events or Terraform apply logs; (5) close DPIA only when all mitigations are verified.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup gdpr_dpia_mitigation_register.csv
| rename mitigation_task as task_number
| join task_number [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_task" earliest=-180d
    | eval done=if(state IN ("Closed","Resolved"),1,0)
    | stats latest(state) as state latest(_time) as last_update max(done) as closed by number
    | rename number as task_number
]
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where closed=0 AND age_days > 0
| table processing_activity, mitigation_description, task_number, state, due_date, age_days
| sort - age_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**GDPR DPIA Remediation Monitoring and Mitigation Closure (Art. 35(7)(d))** — The DPIA must include measures to address risks. This use case ties DPIA mitigation items to ITSM work orders, tracks age and overdue status, and verifies technical completion signals (e.g., control deployment logs) where available.

Documented **Data sources**: `gdpr_dpia_mitigation_register.csv`, `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:sc_task"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Add-on for GitHub (Splunkbase 5596). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where closed=0 AND age_days > 0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

This use case does not use a CIM tstats shortcut; the primary SPL above is the source of truth after field normalization in your environment.

Output fields: age_days, closed, done, due_date, last_update, mitigation_description, processing_activity, state.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare ticket and field counts with the ServiceNow ITSM or GRC console (same time range) and confirm synchronization fields and sourcetypes used by this search.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (overdue mitigations), Single value (open high-risk mitigations), Burndown chart (open vs closed), Bar chart (age by owner).

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup gdpr_dpia_mitigation_register.csv
| rename mitigation_task as task_number
| join task_number [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_task" earliest=-180d
    | eval done=if(state IN ("Closed","Resolved"),1,0)
    | stats latest(state) as state latest(_time) as last_update max(done) as closed by number
    | rename number as task_number
]
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where closed=0 AND age_days > 0
| table processing_activity, mitigation_description, task_number, state, due_date, age_days
| sort - age_days
```

## Visualization

Table (overdue mitigations), Single value (open high-risk mitigations), Burndown chart (open vs closed), Bar chart (age by owner).
